behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: logic error in parsing/validation and error construction. Required setup: call parser with invalid strings and assert structured/descriptive errors. MOCK-MISS: behavior is parser-local, not a module handoff. Rejected higher levels: no behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess (System), real AI output (Agentic), or multi-agent sequencing (Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven parser tests over invalid cron strings; assert reject/accept, exact error category, and message content includes offending field/value.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard and edge cron semantics (last day of month, nearest weekday).
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: calendar algorithm/logic error in next-fire computation. Required setup: deterministic input cron + start timestamp + expected sequence oracle. MOCK-MISS: this behavior can fail within the calculator alone; no required module wiring. Rejected higher levels: no concrete need for real infra, AI model output, or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Deterministic sequence tests for multiple cron patterns including month-end and nearest-weekday rules, asserting exact next N timestamps from fixed start times.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedules firing within minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error between schedule expansion outputs and overlap-window computation/reporting. Required setup: wire schedule objects + next-run expansion + detector and verify emitted conflict windows. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no AI-quality dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejected System because behavior text only requires local schedule interactions, not real external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Compose two real schedule objects with a 5-minute min-gap, generate runs over a horizon, run overlap detection, and assert exact conflicting intervals and involved schedules.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module interaction error between cron evaluation and timezone/DST resolution rules. Required setup: scheduler components using real timezone library/rules in-process across known DST transition instants. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required subprocess/real network/OS primitive beyond local library data, (b) no AI dependency, (c) no multi-step agentic flow. Rejected System since behavior text does not require external system interaction; local module wiring with real TZ rules is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: End-to-end in-process scheduling test for a DST-observing timezone: assert expected handling for nonexistent 2:30 AM in spring-forward and duplicated 2:30 AM in fall-back (policy-defined choice).

behaviors.description: When next run is already in the past, engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill).
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/state-policy error between computed schedule state and policy executor behavior. Required setup: wire calculator output, current-time reference, and policy configuration to observe resulting decision/actions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; controllable clock/input suffices, (b) no AI dependency, (c) no agentic chain. Rejected System because downtime is represented by timestamps/state, not required real infrastructure failure.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate last-seen/now such that next run is in the past; run engine with `skip` and `backfill` policies and assert emitted execution plan (none vs queued missed instances).
